title: Level 809
wikitext:
[[File:Level 809.png|600px|center|link=]]

{{Alt Class |no=809 |ext=3 |env=5 |ent=0 }} {{Background|file=Level 809.png}}
'''Level 809''' is the 810th level of the Backrooms and one of the smallest known levels. It is believed to possess a direct connection to [[Level 7]] and can rarely be observed from its sky as a distant luminescent object.

===
Overview
===
'''Level 809''' consists of a stable and finite planetary environment with a spherical shape, a radius of approximately 1 kilometer, and a surface area of roughly 12.6 km². The level maintains both meteorological activity and angular momentum, forming a self-contained geographical topology. The surface of the planet is composed of crystalline salt lakes arranged in numerous layers, which are estimated to extend as far as the planetary core. Although the salts exhibit structural variations, the majority possess a concentration primarily composed of sodium chloride. The terrain retains an exceptionally flat structure, producing an almost perfectly smooth topological profile. The mechanism by which the planet preserves its integrity remains unknown. Despite its relatively small mass, the level consistently maintains a gravitational acceleration of exactly 10 m/s², while the structure itself does not fracture under its own weight. Furthermore, a continuous convection of liquid hydrochloric acid has been observed rising from deeper layers toward the surface. During exploration, this phenomenon may result in sudden acidic eruptions emerging from the ground, capable of inflicting fatal injuries. Weather conditions remain constant at a temperature of 15°C. Although uncommon, evidence of a functioning water cycle can occasionally be observed. Rare precipitation events may trigger violent reactions with the hydrochloric acid present on the surface, making affected areas potentially lethal. Due to the level's limited surface area, however, avoiding hazardous weather conditions is generally possible. The level presents significant challenges for exploration. No sustainable food source has been documented within the environment. However, aside from environmental hazards, most fatalities are attributed to severe dehydration occurring within only a few hours. Due to the anomalous properties of the level, an individual who has remained within Level 809 for merely two hours will exhibit dehydration comparable to that of a person deprived of fluids for approximately two days under normal circumstances. Although the planet's exact location within the Backrooms remains unknown, it can be observed from the open sky of [[Level 7]] with the aid of a telescope. Additionally, the planet continuously produces a vibration measuring exactly 809 Hz through its rotation, atmospheric movement, and the motion of its internal layers. This frequency directly corresponds to the level's [[Object 2|Level Key]]. Prolonged exposure may result in muscular fatigue and loss of physical strength among wanderers. The level is devoid of entities. No entities have ever been documented within the environment. Furthermore, observations indicate that only a single wanderer may exist within the level at any given time. Despite this, the level possesses no known variants. Entry occurs when a wanderer within [[Level 7]] becomes completely covered by drifting salt crystals. Once transportation has occurred, the salt crystal anomaly will not manifest again until the individual either exits the level or dies. Located within the planet's northern regions is the only structurally distinct location known within the level: a factory. Although the structure appears abandoned and heavily damaged from the exterior, operational generators, conveyor systems, and machinery can still be found inside. Most industrial equipment present consists of generic inventory with no clearly identifiable function. While the factory provides protection from many external hazards, it suffers from the same scarcity of resources present throughout the rest of the level. Some reports claim that packaged food products may rarely appear within storage cabinets, although these claims remain unconfirmed. In addition, the factory contains numerous metal containers filled with liquid sodium hydroxide. The purpose of these containers is unknown, though leaks have occasionally been observed, creating localized hazards within the facility. Exiting the level is not particularly difficult in terms of methodology; however, surviving long enough to reach the factory represents the primary challenge. Rare freezer rooms located within the facility may serve as exits. Activating their cooling systems and remaining inside will cause the individual to lose consciousness within a short period, after which they will awaken in [[Level 908]].
